steps.title: Block the shell
steps.description: Lightly sketch a wide dome shape for the shell — about twice as wide as it is tall. This is the largest shape and everything else will fit around it.

steps.title: Add the body parts
steps.description: Sketch a small oval for the head poking out the front, two front legs, two back legs, and a short tail at the back. Use the shell as your unit of measure.

steps.title: Outline confidently
steps.description: Trace the silhouette of the whole turtle in one continuous outline. Make sure the legs appear to come from under the shell — show the overlap.

steps.title: Add the shell pattern
steps.description: Draw a horizontal curve across the shell about a third of the way down — this is where the upper plates begin. Add 5–6 hexagonal segments below it.

steps.title: Detail the face
steps.description: Add a small eye, a short line for the mouth, and a couple of nostril dots. Keep facial details small — too much detail makes the head feel too big.
